mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 48% in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately depress equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances following the trigger threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during a monetary tightening cycle forces central banks to raise policy rates, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 60% of high-inflation observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 48% during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 52% over 60-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate during a liquidity-constrained regime triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 65% of instances following a 0.25 percentage point rate increase.
